title: Embedding Rat Heart
author: Shaina Robbins
license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.w52fg8e

steps.title: Remove heart from rat immediately following...
steps.instruction: Remove heart from rat immediately following sacrifice Rinse heart in 1X PBS for 00:00:30 to flush blood from the chambers

steps.title: Place heart in the embedding mold...
steps.instruction: Place heart in the embedding mold containing a layer of frozen 100% OCT at bottom. This layer of OCT is to help prevent the heart from sinking to the bottom of the mold exposing the tissue. All areas of the tissue sample should be covered with OCT to allow for optimal sectioning capability.

steps.title: Flash freeze the embedding mold containing...
steps.instruction: Flash freeze the embedding mold containing heart in 100% OCT by suspending mold in either liquid nitrogen or methanol in dry ice Be careful to not allow any liquid nitrogen or methanol to splash into the embedding mold. This will make the OCT become a gooey texture and very difficult to section.

steps.title: Store heart in -80 °C freezer...
steps.instruction: Store heart in -80 °C freezer wrapped in aluminum foil and plastic wrap until the sectioning process is started. Allow heart to sit in -80 °C freezer for at least one day before beginning to section.
